violations.description: Duties of person in charge. — --Person in charge did not ensure employee compliance with an aspect of the food service operation specified in the food service code. When asked about the heating procedure for the soup in the hot hold unit employees stated that the owner, certified food manager, instructed them to put the soups on low so they do not burn. Employees did not mention checking for the proper cooking or reheating temperatures. Soups heated until they reach the right consistency. Certified food manager must assure employees know the proper procedures for heating food items being served. **Soup on display today was reheated and was found at 100F after heating for 2 1/2 hours. Potentially hazardous foods should be reheated to 165F with-in 1 hour, and maintained at 140F or higher while on display.
General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. Observed food employee put on glove without first washing her hands. Hands should be washed before putting on gloves.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. Food items in the top of the refrigerated prep table were not date marked. Potentially hazardous foods should be date marked when held for more than 24 hours.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Slicer was found dirty during inspection. Slicer should be cleaned more thoroughly between uses.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of individual disposable towels, continuous towel supply system, or heated air-drying device. Napkin dispenser next to the hand washing sink was found empty. Napkins should be available at all times for drying hands.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 140F or above during display or service. Tomato soup was found at 100F during inspection. Potentially hazardous foods should be held at 140F or higher.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. Food items in the top section of the refrigerated prep table were found without dates marked. Potentially hazardous foods should be marked when they are held for more than 24 hours. - Observed food items in the bottom of the prep table with labels across the lids. Recommended placing the labels on the side so the date remains if the lids are removed when placed in the top section.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned adequately. Food debris was found dried on the slicer blade. Food contact surfaces should be thoroughly cleaned after each use.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Inside of freezer drawers were found dirty. These should be cleaned and maintained clean.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of individual disposable towels, continuous towel supply system, or heated air-drying device. Napkin dispenser next to the hand washing sink was found empty during inspection. Napkins should be available at all times for drying hands.
Clean dry location, protected (1 pt deducted)
Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
